books.title: Body Sounding Alarms: A Sleep Anxiety Book
books.subtitle: For Managers: When your nervous system scans for danger that isn't there
books.desc: You've been bracing for something you can't name. actually sleep without dreading bedtime.
books.long_desc:
You've been bracing for something you can't name.

This book is for mid-level manager running a cross-functional teams who I'm exhausted but I can't sleep. It doesn't promise to make the feeling stop. It names what's happening in language you can use the next time it happens.

Chapter by chapter, you'll work with the body's false-alarm system and why it keeps firing. Not through exposure or worksheets or morning routines — through recognition, descent, and integration. The practice the book asks for can happen on a subway, between meetings, or at 3am.

You'll walk away knowing what your body is doing and why it makes sense. Not fixed. Not healed in any final sense. But no longer alone with the pattern.

books.desc: You know what you're feeling. That's not enough anymore. release what's been held in my body for years.
books.long_desc:
You know what you're feeling. That's not enough anymore.

This book is for paramedic / firefighter / EMSs who my body holds things my mind has been trying to forget. It doesn't promise to make the feeling stop. It names what's happening in language you can use the next time it happens.

Chapter by chapter, you'll work with the difference between watching and performing watching. Not through exposure or worksheets or morning routines — through recognition, descent, and integration. The practice the book asks for can happen on a subway, between meetings, or at 3am.

You'll walk away knowing what your body is doing and why it makes sense. Not fixed. Not healed in any final sense. But no longer alone with the pattern.
